title: India’s Jio lays out sovereign LEO constellation plan ahead of IPO
summary: Jio Platforms, India's largest telecom company, plans to lease broadband capacity from satellite constellations to establish its own sovereign LEO network ahead of its IPO. The move aims to enhance India's broadband connectivity through a domestic satellite-based infrastructure.

title: MDA Space to buy Blue Canyon Technologies to gain foothold in U.S. market
summary: MDA Space is acquiring smallsat manufacturer Blue Canyon Technologies for $620 million to strengthen its position in the U.S. government market. The deal aims to expand MDA Space's foothold in the lucrative U.S. space industry.

title: ElevationSpace Secures US $40 Million in Series B Funding, Bringing Total Raised to US $63.5 Million
summary: ElevationSpace raised an additional $40 million in Series B funding, bringing its total funding to $63.5 million. The company focuses on space-to-Earth transportation and a space environment utilization and recovery platform.

title: Northrop Grumman says industry ready to scale solid rocket production, with longer contracts
summary: Northrop Grumman states the space industry is prepared to increase solid rocket production, contingent on longer contracts. The company highlights the need for predictable demand from suppliers despite billions in private investment.

title: The MEO durability crisis: why LEO hardware will fail the new orbital economy
summary: The article discusses the challenges of Medium Earth Orbit (MEO) durability compared to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) hardware, highlighting why LEO solutions may not suffice for the evolving orbital economy. It emphasizes the need for more robust technology to handle the demands of MEO.

title: NASA selects mission to study space weather interaction with Earth’s atmosphere
summary: NASA has chosen a new space science mission to investigate how space weather affects Earth's atmosphere. The mission aims to enhance understanding of this interaction.

title: Chinese university-led mission to study asteroid Apophis during close encounter with Earth
summary: A spacecraft developed by Tsinghua University will study asteroid Apophis during its close approach to Earth in 2029. The mission joins international efforts to analyze the asteroid.

title: Mu-g Technologies enters the parabolic flight business
summary: Mu-g Technologies has acquired an aircraft to offer commercial parabolic flight services. This move comes as NASA seeks its own aircraft for reduced-gravity research.

title: Austrian propulsion startup joins sovereign space funding surge
summary: Austrian satellite propulsion startup Gate Space secured 6.3 million euros in funding from Europe's government-backed accelerator program, contributing to Europe's push for greater space sovereignty.

title: EQT to acquire Exolaunch
summary: Private equity firm EQT is acquiring Exolaunch, a rideshare launch services provider for satellites, to address increasing launch demand.

title: Late, great singer-songwriter Elliott Smith finds immortality as an asteroid
summary: An asteroid has been named after late singer-songwriter Elliott Smith, who passed away in 2003 at the age of 34. This posthumous honor immortalizes him in space.

title: Do we need a lunar building code to build moon bases safely?
summary: NASA is advancing plans for a sustained human presence on the moon, with an expert advocating for a lunar building code to ensure safe construction. This initiative aims to address the unique challenges of building infrastructure on the lunar surface.

title: A private company will build and launch NASA's next Mars orbiter in 2028 — and it's not SpaceX
summary: A California-based private company will design, build, and launch NASA's next Mars orbiter in 2028 for the Aeolus mission, marking the first probe to provide daily global environmental measurements of Mars.

title: 'This is not like life that we’ve experienced' 'Star City' cast on struggling to relate to the brutality of Soviet existence in Apple TV's For All Mankind' spin-off
summary: Apple TV+'s For All Mankind spin-off 'Star City' explores the harsh realities of Soviet life, with cast members struggling to relate to its brutality. The show contrasts this with familiar American experiences.

title: 'No one thought it was going to be possible.' A space telescope is falling out of space. This is NASA's daring plan to save it.
summary: NASA's Swift space observatory is falling out of orbit, and the agency is considering a commercial company's proposal to build a spacecraft to save it within nine months. This effort is seen as a daring and unprecedented challenge.

title: Menstruation in space will be studied for 1st time with 'Operation Period'
summary: A new study called 'Operation Period' will investigate menstruation in space for the first time. The research aims to address questions like the '100 tampons' query posed to early female astronauts.

title: Annie Easley, a hero of NASA | Space photo of the day for June 19, 2026
summary: Annie Easley, a pioneering NASA human computer, is featured as the Space Photo of the Day for June 19, 2026. She contributed to the development of the Centaur upper-stage rocket.

title: Juicy new details emerge about an asteroid NASA's Lucy spacecraft flew by last year
summary: NASA's Lucy spacecraft discovered evidence of liquid water altering the asteroid Donaldjohanson, which migrated inward from its original formation farther from the sun.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4920-4926: Surveying the Bands
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover is surveying distinct rock layers on Mount Sharp, each with unique textures and tones, as part of its ongoing mission. The rover's activities are detailed in a blog post by a senior research scientist from the Space Science Institute.

title: NASA Mission to Study Space Weather Impacts of Earth’s Atmosphere
summary: NASA selected the DAPHNE mission to study how space weather affects Earth's atmosphere and impacts technology like GPS and satellites. The mission will proceed to Phase B of development.

title: NASA Awards Contract for Commercial Satellite Data Acquisition
summary: NASA has selected eight new companies and six existing contractors to acquire commercial satellite data products, expanding available high-resolution and frequent observations for research and decision-making.

title: From Suriname to Space: Rohit Goeptar Shares His Journey to NASA
summary: Rohit Goeptar shares his inspiring journey from a poor family in Suriname to working at NASA, highlighting his move to California and back to South America during his childhood.

title: Desert Field Test With NASA Advanced Rover Prototype
summary: NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory tested a prototype rover named ERNEST in the Colorado Desert in March 2026. The rover, designed for advanced mobility and robotic autonomy, served as a testbed for new autonomy software.

title: NASA Testing Advanced Capabilities for Moon, Mars Rovers
summary: NASA is testing the ERNEST rover in the Colorado Desert to advance robotic autonomy and navigation for future Moon and Mars missions. The prototype recently traveled 16 miles with minimal human intervention.

title: NASA’s Lucy Reveals Wobbling, Peanut-Shaped Asteroid
summary: NASA's Lucy spacecraft discovered that the asteroid Donaldjohanson is wobbly and peanut-shaped, formed from fragments after a violent collision 155 million years ago.

title: Stages of Star Formation
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope captured an image showing various stages of star formation in the Orion Molecular Clouds. The image highlights stellar embryos and protoplanetary discs in a filament of cold gas and dust.

title: Hubble Glimpses Merging Galaxy Clusters
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of galaxy cluster CL0016+1609 (MACS J0018.5+1626), revealing it as two merging clusters observed along the line of sight. This cluster is one of the most studied at X-ray and radio wavelengths.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is planning repairs for the Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. This marks another busy month for the crew aboard the station.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space has transported the second stage of its Terran R heavy-lift rocket to the Stennis Space Center, marking progress toward its planned 2026 debut. This development highlights Relativity Space's ongoing efforts to advance its next-generation launch vehicle.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten space launches are scheduled globally in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for the space industry. The launches will take place from various sites around the world.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider is based in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed its cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. The focus remains on the progress following Flight 12.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, and LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing. The month is expected to feature three missions for a single internet constellation.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. The mission marks a significant milestone for Japan's space program after previous setbacks.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification structural verification for the first stage of its Nova rocket. This milestone marks progress in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission, which will test lunar landers in low Earth orbit (LEO). The crew consists of veteran astronauts assigned to this historic mission.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase team has begun cryogenic testing on Booster 20 in preparation for Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news. No specific details about the content were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: A new advanced robotic arm is being developed for space applications, aiming to enhance robotic operations in orbit. This technology could significantly improve satellite servicing and maintenance tasks.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports European space science activities by involving research institutes.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping ESA test and develop materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space for its missions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with fresh ideas from emerging talent.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space missions and education.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry event involving a parachute test, likely for a Mars or planetary mission, was conducted under high-temperature conditions to simulate entry, descent, and landing scenarios. The bake-out process ensures the parachute's integrity and performance in extreme environments.

title: Boeing Completes Quantum Lab Test Ahead of 2027 Flight
summary: Boeing successfully demonstrated quantum entanglement swapping in ground tests for its Q4S quantum networking satellite, aiming for a 2027 flight. The company's quantum lab test marks a key milestone ahead of the satellite's deployment.

title: True Anomaly Declares Mission X-3 Success
summary: True Anomaly announced the successful completion of Mission X-3 with its autonomous orbital vehicle, Jackal, achieving all test objectives and being fully commissioned.

title: Instinct Space Unveils Plans for Low-Cost Lunar Landers
summary: Instinct Space is shifting its focus from navigation support for lunar missions to developing low-cost lunar landers. The company aims to directly contribute to surface missions.

title: Katalyst Raises $12M to Extend Satellite Servicing to GEO
summary: Katalyst secured $12 million in funding to expand its satellite servicing capabilities to geostationary orbit (GEO), with plans for its first GEO mission in 2027. The company is also preparing for a near-term mission launching this month.

title: Dawn Aerospace Raises $25M Series B
summary: Dawn Aerospace has raised $25 million in a Series B funding round to accelerate the development of its Aurora spaceplane and conduct an in-space refueling demonstration.

title: Bill Spotlight: NEW HORIZON Act
summary: A bipartisan bill called the NEW HORIZON Act has been introduced to allow national security missions to utilize commercial orbital data centers. The legislation aims to leverage the growing trend of commercial space-based data infrastructure.

title: Optera Raises £3M to Open New UK HQ
summary: Optera secured £3 million in funding to establish a new UK headquarters, aligning with the UK government's push to enhance domestic technical capabilities.

title: PiLogic Partners with AFRL on AI Anomaly Detection Tech
summary: PiLogic has partnered with the Air Force Research Laboratory (AFRL) to develop AI-based anomaly detection technology, currently being tested on terrestrial flat sats with potential expansion into orbit.

title: Space Stocks Dip After SpaceX IPO
summary: SpaceX's successful IPO led to a decline in shares of other publicly traded space companies, causing a broad pullback among investors. Major players in the space industry saw significant drops following the event.

title: JAXA Nails H3 Return to Flight
summary: JAXA successfully launched the H3 rocket on its eighth mission, marking its first successful flight with a new three-engine variant. The rocket delivered all six of its payloads to space from Japan’s Tanegashima Space Center.

title: Quantum Space to Demonstrate Orbital Refueling Under Pentagon Contract
summary: Quantum Space will demonstrate orbital refueling using its Ranger spacecraft under a Pentagon contract. The company secured the contract from the Department of Defense, as announced on June 18.

title: Iceye Data to Support Flood Monitoring in Norway
summary: The Norwegian Water Resources and Energy Directorate (NVE) has partnered with Iceye to use its satellite data for flood monitoring. This collaboration aims to enhance flood management capabilities in Norway using advanced satellite technology.

title: Vantor and Rheinmetall Plan Joint Venture for Sovereign Space in Germany
summary: Vantor and Rheinmetall have announced plans to form a joint venture aimed at enhancing sovereign space capabilities in Germany and Europe. The collaboration was formalized through a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) signed on June.

title: Private Equity Firm EQT to Acquire Rideshare Provider Exolaunch
summary: Private equity firm EQT is acquiring rideshare provider Exolaunch, marking EQT’s first investment in the space sector. The deal was announced on June 18 by Stockholm-based EQT.

title: Intellian Calls New Production Facility in California a ‘Defining Milestone’
summary: Intellian Technologies is opening a new large-scale manufacturing facility in California, marking a significant milestone for the company. The move is highlighted as a defining achievement by CEO Eric Sung.

title: Lyntris Acquires Robotics Firm Novium to Further Satellite Offerings
summary: Lyntris, a newly formed defense tech company, acquired Houston-based robotics firm Novium to expand its satellite offerings. The acquisition follows Lyntris's formation last month through the merger of Vitesse.

title: Bezos Earth Fund Invests in FireSat Constellation Build-Out
summary: The Bezos Earth Fund is investing $26 million in the FireSat constellation, a wildfire-monitoring satellite initiative. This investment is part of a larger coalition supporting the non-profit Earth.

title: Thursday Morning Conversation With Tina Ghataore
summary: The article features an interview with Tina Ghataore, a former Satellite Executive of the Year, discussing future developments in the space industry. It also includes breaking news related to the interview.

title: Gitai Delays Robotic Servicing Mission to Prioritize SBI Work
summary: Gitai has completed a flight model for a robotic on-orbit servicing mission but is delaying it to prioritize space-based interceptor (SBI) technologies following a recent U.S. Space policy shift.

title: DHS Promotes Latest Space Cybersecurity Research in SPARTA
summary: The Department of Homeland Security's Science and Technology Directorate unveiled new space cybersecurity research under the SPARTA framework to safeguard critical infrastructure. The initiative aims to enhance protection against cyber threats in the space sector.
